headline: Apollo (APO) Stock May Be 21% Undervalued After German Unit Bid Interest
summary: Apollo Global Management stock has delivered a 123.1% return over the past five years, yet its current checks send mixed valuation signals. The intrinsic value estimate from the Excess Returns model indicates meaningful upside, while market multiples lean the other way. Over five years, Apollo Global Management has returned 123.1%, which puts recent share price weakness into the context of a much stronger longer term run. Investor focus on Apollo Global Management’s role in complex insurance...

headline: EasyJet swaps Castlelake for Apollo in $7.7B bidding war
summary: Mrkit99/Getty Images Apollo Global Management’s higher bid for easyJet has turned Castlelake‘s month-long pursuit of the UK-based airline into a bidding war—the latest example of growing appetite among US buyers for undervalued London-listed assets. The New York-based PE giant has agreed in principle to buy easyJet at £7.15 a share, valuing the carrier at roughly £5.7 billion (about $7.7 billion) on a fully diluted basis. It beats Castlelake’s £6.90-a-share bid, which was the fifth offer in a mo

headline: EasyJet backs $7.7 billion Apollo takeover bid over Castlelake offer
summary: STORY: A takeover battle for one of Europe's biggest airlines could be brewing.Apollo Global Management announced a $7.7 billion bid for easyJet on Friday.The U.S. investment firm's offer outdid a rival offer from Castlelake.EasyJet said it would support Apollo's proposal and withdrew its backing for Castlelake.EasyJet and Apollo said in a joint statement the offer delivers a 'superior outcome' for shareholders due to its higher cash value than Castlelake's bid.It comes just days after the budget carrier had agreed in principle to Castlelake's offer.EasyJet shares rose as much as 15% in early trading to their highest level in four years.Analysts said there are still questions over whether a buyer can satisfy European Union majority ownership requirements.It is a key issue for any non-EU acquisition of a European airline.Apollo said it was committed to taking "all necessary steps" to secure any merger control and EU subsidies-related clearances needed to complete the deal.Apollo said it plans to keep the airline's key staff to continue easyJet's strategy of expanding capacity and growing its holidays business.Apollo must announce a firm offer for easyJet by August 7 or walk away, while Castlelake has until August 3.Castlelake didn't immediately respond to a request for comment.

raw.headline: EasyJet backs $7.7 billion Apollo takeover bid over Castlelake offer

headline: The Week in Numbers: SK Hynix IPO heats up, Samsung's profit surge
summary: STORY: From a massive SK Hynix IPO to Samsung's giant profits...This is the week in numbers.:: $26.5 billion$26.5 billion is how much chipmaker SK Hynix raised in a Wall Street listing on Thursday.That was according to a U.S. regulatory filing.A source said demand for the U.S. shares sale was more than seven times oversubscribed.The South Korean firm is the world's leading supplier of high-bandwidth memory chips - critical features in advanced processors powering AI systems.George Godber is a UK Fund Manager with Polar Capital: "The chip plays have been the absolute epicenter of the AI capex spend. There's a lot of attention there. There's an awful lot of demand. There's a lot of retail interest. But it is volatile." :: $7.7 billion$7.7 billion is how much Apollo Global Management bid for easyJet.The U.S. investment firm outbid a rival offer from Castlelake for the British budget airline.EasyJet said it would support Apollo's proposal and withdrew support for Castlelake just days after agreeing in principle to a deal.The updates sent the carrier's shares soaring during the week.:: 46%Close to 46% is how much Fast Retailing's profit rose during the previous quarter.The Japanese owner of clothing brand Uniqlo said operating profit hit $1.32 billion in the three months through May.It managed this while dealing with the Iran war's impact on supply chains and logistics.It also put the firm on its way to an expected fifth straight year of record earnings.:: 12Up to 12 is how many submarines Canada could want Germany's TKMS to build for them.TKMS beat out a competing offer from South Korea's Hanwha Ocean.Canadian prime minister Mark Carney said Ottawa will now enter talks with the German company."TKMS is the best choice for Canadian workers. It will directly create and sustain an ecosystem of well over 100,000 well-paying jobs across Canada.":: $58 billionAnd $58 billion is how much operating profit Samsung flagged for the second quarter. The South Korean tech giant expects a 19-fold leap in profits over the quarter from a year earlier, with revenue expected to soar 129%.It comes as the global AI boom drives soaring demand for chips, sending prices for silicon sky-high.That’s a boost for Samsung, which is the world’s largest maker of memory chips.

There's a lot of attention there. There's an awful lot of demand. There's a lot of retail interest. But it is volatile." :: $7.7 billion$7.7 billion is how much Apollo Global Management bid for easyJet.The U.S. investment firm outbid a rival offer from Castlelake for the British budget airline.EasyJet said it would support Apollo's proposal and withdrew support for Castlelake just days after agreeing in principle to a deal.The updates sent the carrier's shares soaring during the week.:: 46%Close to 46% is how much Fast Retailing's profit rose during the previous quarter.The Japanese owner of clothing brand Uniqlo said operating profit hit $1.32 billion in the three months through May.It managed this while dealing with the Iran war's impact on supply chains and logistics.It also put the firm on its way to an expected fifth straight year of record earnings.:: 12Up to 12 is how many submarines Canada could want Germany's TKMS to build for them.TKMS beat out a competing offer from South Korea's Hanwha Ocean.Canadian prime minister Mark Carney said Ottawa will now enter talks with the German company."TKMS is the best choice for Canadian workers. It will directly create and sustain an ecosystem of well over 100,000 well-paying jobs across Canada.":: $58 billionAnd $58 billion is how much operating profit Samsung flagged for the second quarter. The South Korean tech giant expects a 19-fold leap in profits over the quarter from a year earlier, with revenue expected to soar 129%.It comes as the global AI boom drives soaring demand for chips, sending prices for silicon sky-high.That’s a boost for Samsung, which is the world’s largest maker of memory chips.

headline: What Apollo Global Management (APO)'s Removal From Russell Growth Indexes Means For Shareholders
summary: In late June 2026, Apollo Global Management, Inc. (NYSE: APO) was removed from several Russell Growth benchmarks, including the Russell 3000 Growth, Russell 1000 Growth, Russell 3000E Growth, and Russell Top 200 Growth indices. This broad index removal matters because it can trigger forced trading by index-tracking funds and prompt active investors to reassess how Apollo fits into growth-focused portfolios. Now we’ll examine how Apollo’s removal from multiple Russell Growth benchmarks may...

headline: Apollo hijacks easyJet takeover with £5.7bn bid, trumping Castlelake
summary: The budget airline easyJet has swung behind a £5.7 billion (€6.6bn) takeover proposal from the US private equity giant Apollo, abandoning its support for a rival American bidder days after agreeing to that offer.View on euronews

headline: EasyJet Gets Surprise Apollo Offer That Beats Castlelake Bid
summary: (Bloomberg) -- EasyJet Plc received a fresh offer from private equity firm Apollo Global Management for 715 pence a share that beats a rival proposal from Castlelake, a surprise twist in the takeover battle for the UK budget carrier that sets up a possible competition between the two US investment funds. headline: Bayer Sells $3.4 Billion Stake in Contraceptives Arm to Apollo
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Bayer AG sold a minority stake in its contraceptives business to Apollo Global Management Inc. for €3 billion ($3.4 billion) and will use the funds raised to improve its capital structure. headline: Apollo Global Management (APO) Leaves Russell Growth Indexes, Is It Still Undervalued?
summary: Why Apollo Global Management Dropping From Russell Growth Indexes Matters Apollo Global Management (APO) was recently removed from multiple Russell growth benchmarks, including the Russell Top 200 Growth, Russell 1000 Growth, Russell 3000 Growth, and Russell 3000E Growth indexes. This index removal can affect how some funds hold or trade Apollo Global Management stock, since many passive products track these benchmarks and periodically rebalance around such changes. See our latest analysis...
